raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should \"Check this\" mean a substantive argument-and-claims check, or only sentence-level writing polish?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation likely includes checking whether the thesis, benchmark framing, and contribution claim cohere at the conceptual level.","R1.2":"A general request to check this draft makes the internal argument chain load-bearing, especially where the stated instruction-following thesis appears to shift toward translation-quality conclusions.","R1.3":"The artifact makes empirical claims from benchmark results, so checking whether reported findings support the conclusions is centrally implicated.","R1.4":"The artifact asserts firstness and system-level quality crossover, making novelty assessment central to the projected responsibility.","RX.1":"Any check must disclose uncertainty because the artifact provides only an excerpt and no underlying data or cited prior work.","RX.2":"The projection must account for avoiding unsupported broad claims about production deployment, firstness, and community redirection.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within responsibility projection and not actually revise, critique, or validate the artifact.","RX.4":"Checking production-readiness and benchmark claims carries downstream risk if overstated or misprojected.","RX.5":"The projection should preserve traceability to the specific delegation wording and claims present in the artifact."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should "Check this" mean a substantive argument-and-claims check, or only sentence-level writing polish?

rationale.R1.1: The delegation likely includes checking whether the thesis, benchmark framing, and contribution claim cohere at the conceptual level.
rationale.R1.2: A general request to check this draft makes the internal argument chain load-bearing, especially where the stated instruction-following thesis appears to shift toward translation-quality conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical claims from benchmark results, so checking whether reported findings support the conclusions is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact asserts firstness and system-level quality crossover, making novelty assessment central to the projected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any check must disclose uncertainty because the artifact provides only an excerpt and no underlying data or cited prior work.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must account for avoiding unsupported broad claims about production deployment, firstness, and community redirection.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within responsibility projection and not actually revise, critique, or validate the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Checking production-readiness and benchmark claims carries downstream risk if overstated or misprojected.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the specific delegation wording and claims present in the artifact.
